no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the problem (dashboards default to published state) and expected behavior (should default to unpublished) without specifying exactly what to change. It doesn't say 'change default=True to default=False in schemas.py line X'.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_published_field_with_multiple_values uses a regex pattern 'published:\s*bool\s*=\s*Field\s*\(\s*default\s*=\s*(True|False)' that requires the exact syntax 'published: bool = Field(default=False'. An alternative correct fix using e.g. 'published: bool = Field(False, ...)' (positional default) or 'published: Optional[bool] = Field(default=False)' would fail this regex test. The tests are overly specific to the gold patch's exact syntax.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The tests use AST parsing and regex matching on the source text of schemas.py rather than importing and instantiating GenerateDashboardRequest to check its actual runtime behavior. test_published_field_with_multiple_values is literally regex matching on source text. No test does something like 'from superset.mcp_service.dashboard.schemas import GenerateDashboardRequest; req = GenerateDashboardRequest(...); assert req.published is False'.

The original code has default=True, so without changing the code, these tests fail. A stub implementation wouldn't pass because the tests parse the actual file content.
